title: What do you think Evangelions message was
display_title: What do you think Evangelions message was
wikitext:
[]: What do you think Evangelion's message was?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Sep 08, 2009 6:12 pm What do you think that Evangelion's message is: despair, or hope? By the end of the series, everything has gone south: Rei is dead and replaced with a clone. Asuka is catatonic. Shinji is completely metally destroyed. Kaworu is dead. Kaji is dead. Misato is heartbroken over the above. Ritsuko has finally snapped. Touji is a cripple. Kensuke, Hikari and co. are all gone, and took Pen2 with them. SEELE and Gendo's plans are about to come to fruition. Then EoE happens, and all life on the planet may as well be dead, save Shinji and Asuka. Now then, in what is possibly the series greatest mind screw, Yui says that life may have the chance to come back, if they wish to do so. (Never mind that there is no way she could have possibly predicted what would happen....) Do you think that the ultimate message here was that there is always hope? Or is it that all life has no meaning, and we're better off LCL?

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Tue Sep 08, 2009 6:19 pm i'm gonna go with hope, if an emotionally screwed up 14 year old e.m.o decides that humanity has a chance then i think it just might be so

[]: Re: Whatdo you think Evangelion's message was?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 08, 2009 6:37 pm Series -- break out of your shell EoE -- if you just curl up in a ball, don't be surprised if your world goes to shit around you

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Sep 08, 2009 6:46 pm Stay in school, don't do drugs, become a therapist, and don't rely and teenagers with traumatic pasts to defend the Earth. Seriousnessness: Series: Try not to be afraid of others EoE:"If someone says you are a God say 'yes'!" EDIT: I can also quote Forest Gump. "Life is like a box of chocolates. You never know what you are gonna get."

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Tue Sep 08, 2009 7:06 pm A sort of hopeful despair. The old order has been demolished. Everything that once was has been swept away. Mankind has a chance to experience a new genesis, but only if he chooses to act. This ability of choice and its connected doubt is the cause of the despair.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Sep 08, 2009 7:11 pm Take care of yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by Iam(not)toji » Tue Sep 08, 2009 7:12 pm I think that this is the great thing about Eva, it doesn't really have an ultimate message. Not to use any senior english class cliche's or anything, but it really is the question minus the answer, and it's up to the audience to figure it out for themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Sep 08, 2009 7:32 pm stop watching cartoons and go outside and meet people. Ok for real. Take the risk at getting hurt and let people in because if you don't your not really living.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Sep 08, 2009 9:13 pm When life hands you lemons, make everybody into orange juice.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Sep 08, 2009 10:40 pm Hope. The message the show conveys is hope. That as long as you are alive there is always the chance things could get better, you just have to try and reach for it.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Tue Sep 08, 2009 11:36 pm Always monitor what your children watch.

[]: Re: What do you think Evangelion's message was?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Sep 09, 2009 12:59 am CyberXIII wrote: Or is it that all life has no meaning, and we're better off LCL?

[]: Re: Whatdo you think Evangelion's message was?             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 09, 2009 2:14 am Mr. Tines wrote: Series -- break out of your shell EoE -- if you just curl up in a ball, don't be surprised if your world goes to shit around you

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:47 am When God gives you lemons you FIND A NEW GOD!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Wed Sep 09, 2009 10:50 am The series... You have the right to be here, just because you are - you don't have to deserve a place in the world through deeds and approval.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Sep 09, 2009 12:32 pm Life is a story you're telling yourself -Spanbauer

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 09, 2009 1:00 pm EoTV: Take care of yourself. EoE: I need you. The episode titles for each take of the final (TV series and the film) sum up my personal views of the series as a whole nicely actually. And the titles make sense considering caring for yourself SHOULD come before caring for others. Also of interest is the fact we see episode titles flash across the screen from episode 01 ("Angel Attack") to episode 26 ("The Beast that shouted love at the heart of the world"), "Death", then "Air" during the Komm Susser Tod sequence.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Sep 09, 2009 1:04 pm life sucks. shit happens. you're screwed anyway. but no, really, to me it was something along the lines of "pain lets you know you're alive. deal with it." or, with a slightly more hopeful edge: "you have to go through suffering in order to achieve happiness." to be honest, though, EVA, as amazing as it is, always seemed to me like it was more of an "ok, look here: THIS IS WRONG. do NOT do this" kind of series rather than a "listen, here's how you should act..." one. the actual message was in a negative sense, and you sort of make your own positive from that. as in, it didn't teach me to break out of my shell; instead, it showed me i am inside a shell. i take whatever i want from that, then.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 09, 2009 1:30 pm The moral of the entire story? "Dude, she's like in a coma!"

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 8:50 pm Iam(not)toji wrote: I think that this is the great thing about Eva, it doesn't really have an ultimate message. Not to use any senior english class cliche's or anything, but it really is the question minus the answer, and it's up to the audience to figure it out for themselves.
